stimulus:
Annual Editorials Published in the Riverside Chronicle, 2008–2017

  • 2008: approximately 800
  • 2009: approximately 920
  • 2010: approximately 1,150
  • 2011: approximately 1,250
  • 2012: approximately 1,400
  • 2013: approximately 1,050
  • 2014: approximately 990
  • 2015: approximately 1,080
  • 2016: approximately 760
  • 2017: approximately 400

Editorial writers offer opinion and analysis on public affairs. The graph shows the number of editorials published in the Riverside Chronicle newspaper from 2008 to 2017. According to a media analyst, although the number of editorials published has fluctuated considerably over the period shown, the number overall has fallen since 2008.
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the graph to justify the underlined claim?

answerOptions.content: The greatest annual number of editorials published in the newspaper during the period shown is approximately 1,080 in 2015.
The newspaper published approximately 800 editorials in 2008 and approximately 1,400 in 2012.
The smallest annual number of editorials published in the newspaper during the period shown is approximately 1,400 in 2012.
The newspaper published approximately 800 editorials in 2008 and approximately 400 in 2017.

rationale:
Choice D is correct. Comparing the number of editorials in 2008 (about 800) to 2017 (about 400) demonstrates the overall decline claimed.

Choice A focuses on a single year's peak and 1,080 was not the maximum (1,400 was). Choice B describes an increase from 2008 to 2012, contradicting the claim. Choice C misreads the data — 1,400 in 2012 was the largest, not smallest.
